text.title: Tagged Steps Are the Agent's Contract
text.summary: Every remediation step in a runbook must carry exactly one execution tag that the SRE agent treats as a hard policy.

section.title: Closed tag set on every remediation step
section.p: The runbook spine is fixed: `## Symptoms`, `## Diagnose`, `## Remediate`, `## Escalate`. Diagnose steps are read-only and always agent-runnable. Remediate steps each carry one HTML-comment tag — there is no third option and no default.

rule.statement: Every bullet under `## Remediate` MUST end with exactly one of `<!-- agent:auto -->` or `<!-- agent:approval -->`. The SRE agent MUST execute `agent:auto` steps unattended, MUST refuse `agent:approval` steps until a signed approval token arrives, and MUST abort the runbook with a `runbook_invalid` error when any remediate step lacks a tag.
rule.rationale: A runbook without an explicit tag forces the agent to guess; published Kiro-style production deletions trace to "the runbook said it was OK to run" prose interpretations. A closed tag set makes the safety boundary mechanical.

example.code: ## Remediate - Scale read replicas: `kubectl scale --replicas=+1 sts/pg-replica` <!-- agent:auto --> - Clear stale connections: `psql -c "SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) ..."` <!-- agent:auto --> - Failover primary: `patroni failover` <!-- agent:approval --> - Drop offending table: `psql -c "DROP TABLE audit_2024 ..."` <!-- agent:approval -->
example.note: Each step is unambiguously routed. Agent runs the first two, requests approval for the last two.

example.code: ## Remediate - Restart the database (only if you're sure) - Run failover when latency is high
example.note: No tags, hedged prose. Agent must guess; safe path is to refuse everything, defeating the runbook.

antipattern.description: Adding an implicit "if untagged, treat as auto" default.
antipattern.reason: Defaults turn forgotten tags into silent escalations of agent privilege; the next contributor will write a destructive untagged step and the agent will run it.

section.reference: Runbook skeleton with all four sections.
Kiro incident retro: missing approval boundary on destructive actions.
